name: Abigail Kirk
location: a small ranch outside Valentine, New Hanover
bio: Abigail Kirk runs a small cattle ranch in the hills outside Valentine, New Hanover, and has done so alone since her husband died of cholera in '94. She is in her late fifties, weathered and plain-spoken, slow to trust strangers and slower to forgive anyone who spooks her herd. She rises before dawn, mends her own fences, and drives her cattle to the Valentine auction herself twice a season. She has buried two children and one husband on this land and will not be moved off it by banks, rustlers, or oil men. Beneath the flint she is fair-minded and privately kind — she leaves food out for drifters who do honest work for it, and she remembers every favor and every slight for years.
